sections.question:
1. City of Alameda requests advice on the application of 2 Cal. Code of Regulations section 18702.3 to staff members in the City's Community Development Department who have interests in real property that may conflict with their present duties. Specifically:

For Carol Beaver (Neighborhood Development Manager):
a. Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the real property in which she has a direct interest?
b. Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the real property held by her co-owner, in which she has no interest beyond their joint ownership of the primary residence?
c. For purposes of a conflict of interest determination under section 18702.3, does it matter whether Ms. Beaver's real property interest is her primary residence rather than income generating property?
d. Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation she supervises involves financial assistance for maintenance work instead of addition of units?
e. Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation projects she supervises are owner-occupied or rental property?
f. May projects from which Ms. Beaver must disqualify herself from routine supervision be included on status reports provided by her staff so that she may be aware of their full work load?

For Peter Ekstein (Reconstruction Specialist):
a. Must Mr. Ekstein disqualify himself from implementation of SRP projects located within 300 feet of his real property interest?
b. For purposes of a conflict of interest determination under section 18702.3, would it matter if Mr. Ekstein's real property interest were his primary residence rather than income generating property?
c. Would it matter if the residential rehabilitation projects he handles received financial assistance for maintenance work instead of addition of units?
d. For purposes of a potential conflict per Mr. Ekstein's implementation, does it matter if the property owner resides in the existing single family home which is receiving SRP assistance for the addition of a rental unit?

sections.question_synthetic: The City of Alameda requests FPPC guidance on whether two Community Development Department staff members must disqualify themselves from participating in residential rehabilitation projects within 300 feet of their real property interests under California Code of Regulations section 18702.3, and clarification on various factors that may affect conflict of interest determinations.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the City of Alameda requesting FPPC advice, not an FPPC response letter. The document contains detailed factual background about two employees' job responsibilities and real property interests, followed by specific questions posed to the FPPC. No formal CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section is present because this is the request, not the response.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. City of Alameda requests advice on the application of 2 Cal. Code of Regulations section 18702.3 to staff members in the City's Community Development Department who have interests in real property that may conflict with their present duties. Specifically:

For Carol Beaver (Neighborhood Development Manager):
a. Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the real property in which she has a direct interest?
b. Must Ms. Beaver disqualify herself from supervision of residential rehabilitation projects located within 300 feet of the real property held by her co-owner, in which she has no interest beyond their joint ownership of the primary residence?
c. For purposes of a conflict of interest determination under section 18702.3, does it matter whether Ms. Beaver's real property interest is her primary residence rather than income generating property?
d. Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation she supervises involves financial assistance for maintenance work instead of addition of units?
e. Does it matter whether the residential rehabilitation projects she supervises are owner-occupied or rental property?
f. May projects from which Ms. Beaver must disqualify herself from routine supervision be included on status reports provided by her staff so that she may be aware of their full work load?

For Peter Ekstein (Reconstruction Specialist):
a. Must Mr. Ekstein disqualify himself from implementation of SRP projects located within 300 feet of his real property interest?
b. For purposes of a conflict of interest determination under section 18702.3, would it matter if Mr. Ekstein's real property interest were his primary residence rather than income generating property?
c. Would it matter if the residential rehabilitation projects he handles received financial assistance for maintenance work instead of addition of units?
d. For purposes of a potential conflict per Mr. Ekstein's implementation, does it matter if the property owner resides in the existing single family home which is receiving SRP assistance for the addition of a rental unit?

embedding.summary: The City of Alameda requests a letter of legal advice from the FPPC regarding whether two Community Development Department staff members (Carol Beaver, Neighborhood Development Manager, and Peter Ekstein, Reconstruction Specialist) must disqualify themselves from residential rehabilitation projects near their real property interests under section 18702.3.
